docs.title: Home
docs.text: Sub-pixel displacement tracking for georeferenced and non-georeferenced raster data — developed at the WSL Institute for Snow and Avalanche Research SLF, Davos.

docs.title: MOBI-DIC
docs.text: Multi-purpOse displacement Behavior Identifier Digital Image Correlation Tool

docs.title: Processing modes
docs.text:
• DEM Mode

    3D displacement vectors (EW · NS · vertical) from elevation model pairs or time series using hillshade or orientation image correlation.

    Output: HDF5 + GeoTIFF per component

  • Optical Mode

    2D displacement from multispectral or RGB image pairs or time series.

    Output: HDF5 + GeoTIFF (EW, NS)

  • Image Mode

    2D pixel tracking from any non-georeferenced image pairs or time series (webcam, drone, scanner, …).

    Output: HDF5 + GeoTIFF (pixel displacement)

docs.title: Get started
docs.text:
• Installation

    Install the QGIS plugin and set up the Python environment in a few steps.

    Installation guide

  • First Run

    Configure and run your first DIC job — from input files to displacement map.

    First run

  • QGIS Plugin

    Full reference for all plugin tabs: I/O, DIC parameters, filtering, and results.

    Plugin overview

  • CLI & Batch Processing

    Run and automate batch jobs from the command line with YAML config files.

    CLI reference

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use MOBI-DIC in a publication, please cite:

Oestreicher N., Denzinger F. & Manconi A. (in prep.): MOBI-DIC — applying digital image correlation on elevation models to retrieve 3D surface displacement. WSL/SLF, Davos.

docs.title: Version
docs.text: Field Value Plugin version 1.0.0 QGIS compatibility 3.16 – 4.x Status Beta – Actively Developed

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use MOBI-DIC in your research, please cite:

@misc{slf-dic-2026,
  author = {Denzinger, Florian and Manconi, Andrea},
  title  = {{MOBI-DIC}: A Fast, Sub-Pixel Displacement Tracking Pipeline
             for Remote-Sensing Imagery},
  year   = {2026},
  url    = {https://www.slf.ch},
}

Also cite the underlying algorithms where appropriate:

  • Guizar-Sicairos, M., Thurman, S. T., & Fienup, J. R. (2008). Efficient subpixel image registration algorithms. Optics Letters, 33(2), 156–158.
  • D'Errico, J. R. (2005). Surface fitting using gridfit. MATLAB Central File Exchange.

docs.title: Configuration Reference
docs.text: All three pipelines share the same YAML structure: io, batch, and dic_params sections.

docs.title: io — Input / Output
docs.text: Key Description base_run_tag Prefix for output folder names. Run tags are auto-appended with resolution, window size, and other parameters. dem_primary / img_primary Path to the earlier (reference) DEM or image. dem_secondary / img_secondary Path to the later (target) DEM or image. output_dir Directory where all results are written. Created automatically if it does not exist.

docs.title: batch — Resolution & Window Size
docs.text: Key Description base_dem_res / base_img_res Native resolution of the input data in metres per pixel. Use 1.0 for webcam/pixel mode. target_resolutions List of resampling factors. [1] = native resolution; [2, 4] runs at half and quarter resolution. window_sizes List of DIC correlation window sizes in pixels. Larger windows give smoother but coarser results. E.g. [32, 64, 128].

docs.title: dic_params — Algorithm Parameters
docs.text: Key Type Default Description OR 0 or 1 1 DEM only. 1 = orientation image; 0 = hillshade as the correlation feature. co_f 0 or 1 1 Enable (1) or disable (0) co-registration (global shift correction before DIC). co_os int 2 DFT oversampling factor used during co-registration. coreg_method "full" / "bbox" "full" Co-registration window: full = whole image; bbox = rectangular sub-window. coreg_bbox [minx, miny, maxx, maxy] — Bounding box for co-registration in the input CRS. Required when coreg_method: "bbox". os int 4 DFT oversampling factor for subpixel displacement precision. Higher = more precise but slower. skip_factor int 4 Controls the DIC grid step: step = window_size / skip_factor. Higher values produce denser results. refcode string "EPSG:2056" EPSG code of the input CRS. Use "pixel" for webcam/non-georeferenced mode. auto_reproject_to_utm bool false When true, inputs with a geographic CRS (degrees) are automatically reprojected to UTM before processing. export_sparse_gpkg bool false Export the raw (non-interpolated) DIC point results to a GeoPackage file. rescale_results bool true When true, output GeoTIFFs are at the resampled resolution; when false, outputs are upscaled back to original resolution. align_mode "crop" / "pad" "crop" Webcam only. How to handle inputs of different sizes: crop to the common area, or pad the smaller image with zeros.

docs.title: dic_params.VectorFilter — Post-processing
docs.text:
The vector filter runs after DIC to remove outliers and produce final outputs.

Key Type Description Enabled bool Master switch. Set false to skip post-processing entirely. OutputTxt bool Write filtered vectors to a CSV text file. OutputGpkg bool Write filtered vectors to a GeoPackage. OutputPlot bool Save a displacement vector plot as PNG. PlotResult bool Show an interactive plot window. Set false for batch/headless runs. MaxMagnitude float Exclude vectors with displacement magnitude above this value (map units). ThrMin / ThrMax float Direction thresholds: exclude vectors outside this azimuth range (degrees). ColorMax float Colorbar upper limit on displacement plots (map units). Downsample int Plot every N-th vector arrow (reduces clutter on dense grids). BackgroundAlpha float Opacity of the displacement colour overlay (0–1). HillshadeTif path Optional background raster for plots (DEM or orthoimage). PlotWMS bool Fetch a web map tile background for plots (requires internet). WMSLayer string Tile style: "satellite", "terrain", etc. BlackArrows bool true = black arrows; false = arrows coloured by magnitude.

docs.title: dic_params.AIMask — AI Segmentation Mask (Webcam only)
docs.text:
Removes semantic regions (sky, water, snow, etc.) from results using a pretrained SegFormer model.

Key Description Enabled Master switch. ExcludeTypes List of semantic classes to mask out. Options: sky, water, snow, land, vegetation, buildings, mountain. Device Inference device: null (auto-detect), "cpu", "cuda", or "mps". DilationPixels Expand mask boundaries by N pixels to catch edge fringing. DebugSegmentation Save a false-colour segmentation PNG and log detected class IDs.

docs.title: Complete example
docs.text:
io:
  base_run_tag: Dischma_Aug22
  dem_primary: data/dem_20220801.tif
  dem_secondary: data/dem_20220901.tif
  output_dir: output/

batch:
  base_dem_res: 1.0
  target_resolutions: [1, 2]
  window_sizes: [64, 128]

dic_params:
  OR: 0
  os: 10
  skip_factor: 4
  co_f: 1
  coreg_method: full
  refcode: EPSG:2056
  export_sparse_gpkg: false
  rescale_results: true
  VectorFilter:
    Enabled: true
    MaxMagnitude: 5.0
    ThrMin: 0.02
    ThrMax: 5.0
    OutputGpkg: true
    OutputPlot: true
    PlotResult: false
    ColorMax: 2.0
    Downsample: 4
    BackgroundAlpha: 0.6
    PlotWMS: false
    BlackArrows: false

docs.title: Auto-generated run tags
docs.text:
Output folders are named:

{base_run_tag}_RES{res}_WIN{wi}_SKF{skip}_OR{or}_COF{co_f}[_COREG{method}]

Example: Brienz_RES1.0_WIN64_SKF4_OR0_COF1_COREGfull

docs.title: CLI & Batch Processing
docs.text: The Python CLI processes one or more DIC pairs headlessly, driven entirely by a YAML config file. It is the same code the QGIS plugin calls as a subprocess.

docs.title: Running from a plugin installation
docs.text:
After installing the plugin via QGIS, the DIC scripts and Python environment are available without any separate setup.

The easiest way to get the correct invocation is the 📋 Copy Run Command button in the plugin dialog — it constructs the full command with correct paths from the current dialog state and copies it to the clipboard.

docs.title: Script location
docs.text: Platform Path macOS ~/Library/Application Support/QGIS/QGIS3/profiles/default/python/plugins/slfdic/_dic_scripts/ Linux ~/.local/share/QGIS/QGIS3/profiles/default/python/plugins/slfdic/_dic_scripts/ Windows %APPDATA%\QGIS\QGIS3\profiles\default\python\plugins\slfdic\_dic_scripts\

docs.title: Python interpreter
docs.text: Platform Path macOS / Linux ~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 Windows %USERPROFILE%\.slf-dic\.pixi\envs\default\python.exe

docs.title: Example invocations
docs.text:
# macOS / Linux — DEM-based DIC
~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 \
  ~/Library/Application\ Support/QGIS/QGIS3/profiles/default/python/plugins/slfdic/_dic_scripts/run_dic4dem_processing.py \
  --config /path/to/config_dic4dem.yml

# Optical image DIC
~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 \
  .../slfdic/_dic_scripts/run_dic4optical_processing.py \
  --config /path/to/config_dic4optical.yml

# Webcam DIC
~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 \
  .../slfdic/_dic_scripts/run_dic4webcam_processing.py \
  --config /path/to/config_dic4webcam.yml

# Standalone neighbourhood coherence filter
~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 \
  .../slfdic/_dic_scripts/run_coherence_filter_standalone.py \
  --config /path/to/config.yml

# Standalone iterative neighbour filter
~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 \
  .../slfdic/_dic_scripts/run_neighbour_filter_standalone.py \
  --config /path/to/config.yml

docs.title: Pipeline stages
docs.text:
For each (resolution, window size) combination in the batch sweep:

  1. Load & resample GeoTIFF inputs to the target resolution (rasterio).
  2. Co-register (optional) — compute bulk shift from stable ground and subtract.
  3. Compute feature image — hillshade or orientation image (DEM mode).
  4. Run windowed DFT correlation — src/pixoff.py → src/dftregistration.py.
  5. Interpolate sparse results onto a regular grid (src/gridfit.py).
  6. Write outputs — HDF5 + GeoTIFF per displacement component.
  7. Export sparse points to GeoPackage (optional).
  8. Vector field filter (src/vecfilt.py) — apply thresholds, export PNG/GPKG/TXT.

docs.title: Logging
docs.text: Each run writes a log file run_log_<timestamp>.txt in the output directory. The log includes all parameter values, per-window timing, and any warnings.

docs.title: Using the QGIS Plugin
docs.text:
1. Click the MOBI-DIC toolbar button to open the dialog.
  2. In the I/O tab, set the Primary input (pre-event DEM or image) and Secondary input (post-event).
  3. Choose an Output directory and optionally edit the Run tag.
  4. In the DIC Parameters tab, review the resolution and window size settings. The plugin auto-detects the native resolution from the primary input.
  5. Click ▶ Run DIC to start processing. Output appears in the log panel.
  6. Switch to the Results tab and click Scan results to load the output GeoTIFFs into QGIS.

docs.title: Using the CLI
docs.text:
The easiest way to get the correct command is the 📋 Copy Run Command button at the bottom of the plugin dialog — it builds the full invocation with correct paths from the current settings and copies it to the clipboard.

To run manually, use the Python interpreter and scripts bundled with the plugin:

macOS / LinuxWindows 
~/.slf-dic/.pixi/envs/default/bin/python3 \
  ~/.local/share/QGIS/QGIS3/profiles/default/python/plugins/slfdic/_dic_scripts/run_dic4dem_processing.py \
  --config /path/to/config_dic4dem.yml

%USERPROFILE%\.slf-dic\.pixi\envs\default\python.exe ^
  %APPDATA%\QGIS\QGIS3\profiles\default\python\plugins\slfdic\_dic_scripts\run_dic4dem_processing.py ^
  --config C:\path\to\config_dic4dem.yml

Replace run_dic4dem_processing.py with run_dic4optical_processing.py or run_dic4webcam_processing.py for other modes.

See Configuration Reference for all available parameters.

docs.title: Expected outputs
docs.text:
Each run produces a directory under output_dir/ named after the run tag, containing:

File Description DIC_*.h5 Raw HDF5 results (sparse correlation grid) DIC_*_EW.tif East–West displacement (interpolated GeoTIFF) DIC_*_NS.tif North–South displacement DIC_*_VT.tif Vertical displacement (DEM mode only) DIC_*_CC.tif Cross-correlation coefficient *.gpkg Sparse displacement points (optional) *.png Vector field plot (optional) run_log_*.txt Full processing log

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
System Requirements

Supported Platforms

Platform Architecture Support Windows 10/11 x86_64 Supported macOS 12+ Apple Silicon (M1+) Supported macOS 12+ Intel (x86_64) Supported Linux x86_64 Supported

macOS and Linux are the most tested platforms. Windows is fully supported.

Software Prerequisites

Component Requirement QGIS 3.16 minimum; QGIS 4 recommended — download from qgis.org Internet Required once for downloading the DIC Python environment on first launch

RAM

RAM usage scales with input data size and window size. Approximate peak RAM as a multiple of total input GeoTIFF size (primary + secondary combined):

Window size RAM multiplier 8 ×18.8 16 ×12.0 32 ×10.6 64–512 ×10–10.3
  • 8–16 GB — small datasets (< 1 GB combined) with window sizes ≥ 32
  • 32–64 GB — recommended for typical datasets (1–5 GB combined)
  • 128 GB+ — large datasets (> 10 GB combined) with small windows (8–16)

If RAM is limited, increase the window size or reduce the target resolution.

Disk Space: ~5 GB for the DIC Python environment (downloaded automatically on first launch).

docs.title: Step 1: Install the plugin
docs.text:
1. Open QGIS
  2. Go to Plugins → Manage and Install Plugins
  3. Switch to the Install from ZIP tab
  4. Browse to the _plugin.zip file you received
  5. Click Install Plugin
  6. In the Installed tab, find MOBI-DIC and tick the checkbox to enable it

A toolbar button (MOBI-DIC logo) will appear in the QGIS toolbar.

docs.title: Step 2: Install the DIC Python environment
docs.text:
The plugin needs a dedicated Python environment with the DIC processing libraries. This is downloaded automatically on first launch — no terminal or administrator rights are required.

  1. Click the MOBI-DIC toolbar button to open the dialog
  2. Click the Settings button (⚙) in the top-left of the dialog
  3. In the DIC Python Environment section, click Install DIC Environment

4. Review the package summary and click Install 5. Wait for the installation to complete — this typically takes a few minutes. Progress is shown in the dialog.

After installation the status line shows ✓ Installed and the ▶ Run DIC button becomes available.

Environment location

The environment is stored in ~/.slf-dic/ (macOS/Linux) or %USERPROFILE%\.slf-dic\ (Windows) and survives plugin updates.

docs.title: QGIS Plugin Overview
docs.text: The MOBI-DIC QGIS plugin provides a full graphical interface for the DIC processing pipeline. It is a standalone dialog plugin (no Processing provider) that runs the Python processor as a subprocess and communicates results back through the QGIS layer tree.

docs.title: Dialog layout
docs.text:
The dialog is organised into four tabs, plus a collapsible log panel at the bottom:

Tab Purpose I/O Input files, output directory, run tag, single-pair or time-series mode DIC Parameters Resolution sweep, window sizes, co-registration, feature type Vector Filter Post-processing thresholds, GPKG/PNG export Results Scan and load output GeoTIFFs, rename layers, Google Earth export

docs.title: Toolbar buttons
docs.text: Button Action Load Config… Load a previously saved YAML config file (Ctrl+O) Save Config… Save all current dialog settings to a YAML file (Ctrl+S) 📋 Copy the equivalent shell command to the clipboard ▶ Run DIC Start processing (enabled when both inputs are set) ■ Cancel Interrupt the running subprocess

docs.title: DIC Parameters Tab
docs.text: Controls the core algorithm settings. These map directly to the batch and dic_params sections of the YAML config.

docs.title: Resolution & window size
docs.text: Parameter Description Base resolution (m) Native pixel size; auto-detected from the primary input CRS + GeoTIFF metadata. Target resolutions Comma-separated list of resolutions (in metres) to process. The primary input is resampled to each. Window sizes Comma-separated list of correlation window sizes (pixels). Larger windows are more robust but coarser. Skip factor Grid step between correlation windows (pixels). 1 = dense grid; 2 = every other pixel.

docs.title: Correlation feature (DEM mode)
docs.text:
OR flag Feature computed 0 Hillshade — illumination image from DEM (azimuth 315°, elevation 45° by default) 1 Orientation — aspect/gradient direction image

Hillshade works best for smooth topography; orientation is more robust when illumination angles change between acquisitions.

docs.title: Co-registration
docs.text: Parameter Description Enable co-registration (co_f) Subtract a bulk shift computed from stable ground before DIC. Method full — use the whole image overlap; bbox — restrict to a bounding box drawn on canvas. Co-reg bounding box Four spinboxes (Xmin, Ymin, Xmax, Ymax) in the project CRS. Use Draw on Canvas to define interactively.

docs.title: Oversampling
docs.text: os (oversampling factor, default 10) controls sub-pixel precision in the DFT registration step. Higher values give finer sub-pixel resolution at the cost of compute time. Values between 10 and 100 are typical.

docs.title: CRS
docs.text: Detected automatically from the primary input. Shown as an EPSG code (default EPSG:2056 – Swiss LV95 for SLF data). Change it in the Settings dialog if needed.

docs.title: I/O Tab
docs.text: The I/O tab configures input files, output location, and the run mode (single pair vs. time series).

docs.title: Single-pair mode
docs.text: Field Description Primary input Pre-event DEM or image (GeoTIFF). Sets the reference CRS and pixel size. Secondary input Post-event DEM or image (GeoTIFF). Must overlap spatially with the primary. Output directory Directory where result sub-folders are written. Run tag Short label prepended to output file names. Auto-generated from parameters if left blank.

docs.title: Time-series mode
docs.text:
Switch to Time Series mode to process multiple pairs in sequence. A table lists the pairs, each with an automatically generated tag derived from file names (date detection: YYYYMMDD or YYYY-MM-DD patterns).

Use the + / − buttons to add or remove pairs. The order in the table determines the processing sequence.

docs.title: Output naming
docs.text:
Run tags are auto-generated following this pattern:

{base_tag}_RES{res}_WIN{wi}_SKF{skip}_OR{or}_COF{co_f}[_COREG{method}]

For example: MyRun_RES1.0_WIN64_SKF1_OR0_COF1_COREGfull

docs.title: Signals
docs.text:
The I/O tab emits two signals that other tabs listen to:

  • output_dir_changed — triggers a results scan in the Results tab.
  • primary_input_changed — triggers auto-detection of resolution and CRS in the DIC Parameters tab.

docs.title: Results Tab
docs.text: Scans the output directory for processed GeoTIFFs and loads them into QGIS with optional auto-symbology.

docs.title: Scanning results
docs.text: Click Scan for Results to find all DIC_*.tif files under the selected output directory. The scan also triggers the range computation for the Symbology table (see below).

docs.title: Short-name renaming
docs.text:
Enable Auto-rename layers to replace the full file name with a compact label:

YYYY-MM-DD COMP

For example: DIC_20230801_20230901_RES1.0_WIN64_x.tif → 2023-08-01 X

docs.title: Symbology & Display
docs.text:
Each component has a configurable colormap, Min/Max range, and Range mode:

Component Default colormap Notes X, Y, Z, dZ RdBu (diverging) Range centred at zero XY, XYZ Deformation QML Magnitude — always ≥ 0 AZ AZ bundled QML Fixed 0–360° DIP DIP bundled QML Fixed −90–90° EE YlOrRd Fixed 0–1

docs.title: Range mode
docs.text:
Each row has an individual Range combo (Auto / Fixed):

  • Auto — computes Min/Max from the raster data using configurable percentiles (default 5th–95th). Symmetric components (X, Y, Z, dZ) are automatically centred at zero: bound = max(|p5|, |p95|), range set to [−bound, +bound]. AZ, DIP and EE always remain at their fixed defaults regardless of this setting.
  • Fixed — uses the Min/Max values entered in the spinboxes.

The default range mode and percentile bounds are configured globally under Settings → Results (see Global Settings).

docs.title: Google Earth export
docs.text:
Select one or more layers in the list and click Export to KMZ to create a georeferenced overlay. Options include:

  • TimeSpan — embed acquisition dates so layers animate in Google Earth's timeline.
  • Single KMZ per layer or Master KML linking all layers.

Tip

Use the master KML option when exporting a full time series — it keeps file sizes small and lets you toggle layers in Google Earth.

docs.title: Global Settings
docs.text:
The Settings → Results section controls default behaviour applied on every scan:

Setting Default Description Default range mode Auto Whether Min/Max are computed from data or taken from fixed defaults Percentile range 5 % – 95 % Low/high percentile bounds used in Auto mode Custom default ranges (table) Per-component Min/Max used when Fixed mode is active; Reset button restores built-in defaults

Range computation uses GDAL's histogram method — no full raster read is needed; overviews are used where available.

docs.title: Vector Filter Tab
docs.text: Post-processing step applied after the DIC correlation grid has been interpolated. Filters outliers and exports visual outputs.

docs.title: Filter thresholds
docs.text: Parameter Description Max magnitude Discard vectors whose total displacement magnitude exceeds this value (metres). Min threshold Discard vectors below this magnitude (removes noise). Max threshold Upper bound (same as max magnitude but applied independently per component). Direction filter Optionally restrict to vectors within ± tolerance of a reference azimuth.

docs.title: Outputs
docs.text: Option Description Export GPKG Write filtered sparse vectors to a GeoPackage file. Export TXT Write filtered sparse vectors as a plain-text CSV. Export PNG Render a vector field overlay on a background map and save as PNG.

docs.title: Background map
docs.text:
When PNG export is enabled, a basemap tile (OpenStreetMap or custom URL) is fetched and used as the background. Requires internet access or a local tile server.

Note

The vector filter can also be re-run on existing results without reprocessing the full DIC pipeline — use the Post-Processing tab in the plugin.
